text.title: surveys Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for surveys as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Double-barreled: "How satisfied are you with the speed and accuracy of search results?"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two dimensions in one question — a user who finds search fast but inaccurate cannot give a meaningful answer. Split into two separate questions.

failure-mode.description: Leading: "How much do you love our new feature?"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Presupposes positive sentiment; drives respondents toward positive answers regardless of actual experience. Neutral wording: "How would you rate the new feature?"

failure-mode.description: Vague: "How often do you use it?"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "It" is undefined; "often" is undefined. Every respondent interprets the question differently, producing incomparable data.

failure-mode.description: Double negative: "Is there anything you don't dislike about the onboarding?"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Double negatives require cognitive parsing effort that introduces noise and errors in responses. Rewrite as a direct positive or negative question.

failure-mode.description: Self-report bias: stated preferences differ from revealed behavior; survey results need triangulation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users often report different behaviors than they actually exhibit. Surveys must be paired with behavioral analytics for validation.

failure-mode.description: Survey fatigue: long surveys collapse response rate and inflate straight-lining (same answer all the way down).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fatigue introduces systematic error. Keep surveys under 5 minutes and pilot-test timing.

failure-mode.description: Sampling bias: who answers is rarely who you mean. Voluntary samples skew engaged + dissatisfied.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Survey respondents are self-selected. Compare respondent demographics to your user base to assess bias.

failure-mode.description: Agent invents a 7-point Likert scale when a 5-point was specified.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Scale changes invalidate benchmark comparability; constrain the agent to the exact scale defined in the research plan.

failure-mode.description: Agent codes open-ended responses and assigns confident themes to very short responses ("ok", emoji, single word).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs hallucinate themes from sparse text; enforce a minimum word count before LLM coding and flag low-confidence items for human review.

failure-mode.description: Agent computes NPS or SUS via LLM completion rather than deterministic code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs make arithmetic errors and confuse metric formulas; use the provided Python calculator for all metric computations.

failure-mode.description: Agent treats high survey NPS as a signal that users will pay for the product.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stated preferences overestimate willingness-to-pay by 2-4x; willingness-to-pay research requires separate conjoint or pricing studies, not a satisfaction survey.
